question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user produce a dataset where `recordFileRepository.findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd())` returns `Optional.empty()` prematurely due to a gap in record file timestamps, causing the iterator to terminate early and leave large amounts of data unpruned, eventually causing an out-of-disk DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `synchronized` keyword on `prune()` to cause a thread starvation DoS by ensuring the method never returns (e.g., by generating a dataset that causes an infinite loop in `hasNext()`), blocking the single Spring scheduler thread and preventing all other `@Scheduled` tasks in the importer from executing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the `topic_message` table via HCS (Hedera Consensus Service) message submissions, causing `TopicMessageRepository.prune(endTimestamp)` to execute a DELETE of billions of rows per batch, generating excessive PostgreSQL autovacuum work that degrades overall database performance and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where `current == max` reference equality check fails because the JPA/Hibernate session returns a different object instance for the same record file, causing the iterator to never terminate and the `prune()` method to loop indefinitely, consuming all CPU resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user generate a workload where the `counters` TreeMap in `RecordFileIterator` accumulates entries for thousands of dynamically named tables, causing `counters.values().stream().reduce(0L, Long::sum)` to perform O(n) work on every single batch iteration, cumulatively consuming excessive CPU and causing the retention job to run for an unbounded duration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a circuit breaker or maximum runtime limit on the `prune()` method to cause it to run for days on a sufficiently large dataset, holding the `synchronized` lock and preventing any other thread from calling `prune()`, effectively disabling the retention mechanism and causing unbounded storage growth?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user produce record files with `consensusEnd` values that cause `current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod` to wrap around to a value less than `current.getConsensusEnd()`, causing `endTimestamp >= max.getConsensusEnd()` to be false when it should be true, and the iterator to make an unbounded number of `findNextBetween()` calls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user generate a large number of `crypto_transfer` records via HBAR transfers, causing `CryptoTransferRepository.prune(endTimestamp)` to execute a DELETE that acquires row-level locks on the `crypto_transfer` table for an extended period, blocking all balance-related queries on the mirror node REST API and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `prune()` logs `iterator.getCounters()` and `iterator.getStopwatch()` on every exception, and by generating a dataset that causes thousands of exceptions per run, flood the logging system with gigabytes of log output that exhausts disk space and causes a non-network DoS of the entire mirror node host?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user produce a dataset where `findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd())` returns a record file with `consensusEnd` equal to `max.getConsensusEnd()`, causing `current == max` to be true on the very first call to `hasNext()` after initialization, but the prune for that single batch deletes all data in one unbounded transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user generate a workload that causes `transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult()` to throw a `TransactionException` on every batch, causing the outer `try/catch` in `prune()` to catch it and log it, but the iterator state is left inconsistent, causing subsequent calls to `prune()` to re-process the same batches indefinitely and perform redundant DELETE operations that degrade database performance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `initialDelay = 120_000` (2 minutes) combined with a very large dataset to ensure that the first `prune()` run after startup takes longer than the JVM's available heap, causing an `OutOfMemoryError` that is caught by the generic `Exception` handler, logged, and then the scheduler re-invokes `prune()` after the frequency delay, repeatedly causing OOM crashes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user produce a dataset where the gap between consecutive record file `consensusEnd` values is always exactly `batchPeriod - 1` nanoseconds, causing the iterator to make one `findNextBetween()` database call per record file (potentially millions), exhausting the database connection pool and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user generate a large number of `entity_transaction` records, causing `EntityTransactionRepository.prune(endTimestamp)` to execute a DELETE that generates excessive PostgreSQL WAL traffic, filling the WAL disk partition and causing the entire PostgreSQL instance to halt with a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTableName()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `Utility.toSnakeCase()` throws an unchecked exception for a specific class name pattern, causing `getTableName()` to propagate the exception through `retentionRepositories.forEach()`, rolling back the current transaction and leaving the database in a partially pruned state that causes inconsistency-related DoS on dependent services?
